scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The verbatim describes Eudoxus in Egypt at the court of Mausolus, following local priestly customs.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Eudoxus of Cnidus lived c. -390 to -337; he visited Egypt and the court of Mausolus around -360.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Heraclitus was an Ephesian and his maxims reflect the civic obligations of the Ephesian citizen body.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Heraclitus of Ephesus flourished around the 69th Olympiad (-504 to -501).

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule concerns the hereditary office of the Basileus in Ephesus, which Heraclitus' family held.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Heraclitus flourished c. -500 and famously renounced his hereditary title at this time.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The verbatim explicitly names 'the Ephesians' as the community exercising the power of banishment.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Relates to the banishment of Hermodorus, a contemporary and friend of Heraclitus (fl. -500).

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Melissus was a Samian statesman and general (admiral) of the Samian fleet.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Melissus commanded the Samian fleet against Athens in the Samian War of -441.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Parmenides is described as serving his native city, Elea, as a legislator.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Parmenides is typically dated to the first half of the 5th century BCE; -475 is a representative midpoint of his activity.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The event involves Pythagorean communities across the Greek world acting to free Xenophanes.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Xenophanes of Colophon lived c. -570 to -475; -500 represents his floruit and period of wandering.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule describes education provided by Persian Magians left by King Xerxes in Abdera.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Xerxes invaded Greece in -480, which is when he entertained Democritus' father according to tradition.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The law and its application to Democritus occur within the context of his native city, Abdera.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Democritus flourished c. -420; the event involves his defense against the law after returning from travels.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The event involves Democritus' sister and her ritual obligations in their home city.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Democritus died at a very advanced age, traditionally around -370.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The verbatim explicitly identifies the Athenians as the group that expelled Protagoras.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Protagoras was reportedly prosecuted for impiety and expelled from Athens c. -415.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Protagoras' professional activity and fee-setting were centered in the Athenian intellectual market.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Protagoras began his career as a sophist in the mid-5th century BCE; -440 represents his peak influence.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The decree was voted by the city of Elis to honor their citizen Pyrrho.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Pyrrho of Elis lived c. -360 to -270; -300 represents his period of high esteem in his city.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule describes the granting of citizenship by the Athenian state.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: The assassination of Cotys I occurred in -360; the subsequent grant of citizenship to the slayers (attributed here to Pyrrho) followed shortly after.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The verbatim refers to the customs and internal laws of the Persians.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Diogenes Laertius is documenting the tenets of Pyrrhonism (c. -300) which used these cultural comparisons.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The verbatim refers to the social customs of the Massagetae, a nomadic Scythian people.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Diogenes cites Eudoxus' 'Voyage round the World', written c. -360.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule describes the customary practices of the Cilician people.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Diogenes is citing a historical generalization of Cilician piracy, which was at its height in the 2nd century BCE.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: Timon lived and raised his children in Elis under the influence of Pyrrho.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Timon of Phlius lived c. -320 to -230; his time in Elis with his children was around -275.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The encounter takes place in an Athenian market between two philosophers active in Athens.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Arcesilaus and Timon were contemporaries in Athens in the early 3rd century BCE.

scores.rule_polity_reasoning: The rule describes the social composition of Epicurus' School (The Garden) in Athens.
scores.rule_time_reasoning: Epicurus established his school in Athens in -306; -300 represents its early operation.
